# Count unique RDP targets and crown-jewel hits via if-in-aggregate (T1021.001)

**Dataset**: `gx_06_rdp`

Threshold-style lateral-movement detector: `dedup` reduces to one row per (user, dst_host), an inner join to `gx_06_hosts` attaches the asset `tier`, and the per-user rollup counts unique targets while a `count_distinct(if(tier = "crown-jewel", dst_host, null))` counts *only* crown-jewel targets in a single aggregate pass. The `filter unique_targets >= 2` keeps users that fanned out across at least two hosts. Technique notes: the joined `tier` is referenced by **bare name** (not `h.tier`), and an `if(...)` returning `null` inside `count_distinct` is the idiom for conditional distinct counting.

```sql
dataset = gx_06_rdp
| dedup user, dst_host by asc ts
| join type = inner (dataset = gx_06_hosts) as h h.host = dst_host
| alter tier_flag = if(tier = "crown-jewel", concat("CJ:", dst_host), dst_host)
| comp count_distinct(dst_host) as unique_targets, values(tier_flag) as targets, count_distinct(if(tier = "crown-jewel", dst_host, null)) as crown_targets by user
| filter unique_targets >= 2
| fields user, unique_targets, crown_targets, targets
| sort desc crown_targets, desc unique_targets
```
